mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly reducing present values of future cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and elevated recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, invalidating the recession risk channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation and slowing employment growth creates stagflationary pressure, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production indicates rapid supply-side recovery, boosting short-term market sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of positive INDPRO growth months, or signal fails to beat buy-and-hold in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal spending reflects robust household demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 305.0, persistent inflation forces aggressive central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of high-CPI observations, or tightening expectations fail to translate into equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Interaction signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or joint labor strength fails to predict equity upside.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of rising HOUST observations, or signal loses predictive power during housing booms.
